title: Improved Export to Excel with Resourcing Boards
excerpt: When exporting a resource workload board to excel, the export is extremely difficult to use for leveraging Excel’s data analysis tools. I am unable to make any use of the data the way it exports. Would be nice to get dat…

posts.author: dlawrencer24 (Dan Lawrence)
posts.cookedHtml: When exporting a resource workload board to excel, the export is extremely difficult to use for leveraging Excel’s data analysis tools. I am unable to make any use of the data the way it exports. Would be nice to get data in numeric form by resource to allow you to manipulate the data.

posts.author: RayaSlavova (Raya Slavova)
posts.cookedHtml: We desperately need this feature as well. We report all the project hours to finance but the excel report doesn’t have any numeric values. Because of it we need to use a separate tool for resource allocation and we cannot utilize the workload view on Monday

posts.author: Iryna_Belaya (Iryna Belaya)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello @RayaSlavova and @dlawrencer24
If you are open to apps, I can suggest to try Smart Spreadsheet for monday. The app exports groups and subitems on different tabs which makes it easier to analyze data.
